steps.title: Infinitely Many Solutions Means the Equations Are Proportional
steps.content:
When a system has infinitely many solutions, the two equations represent the same line. This means the second equation is a scalar multiple of the first:

$$gx + ky = c\left(\frac{2}{5}x + \frac{7}{5}y\right)$$

for some nonzero constant $c$.

steps.title: Set Up and Solve for the Coefficient Ratio
steps.content:
Expanding the right side gives:

$$gx + ky = \frac{2c}{5}x + \frac{7c}{5}y$$

Matching coefficients: $g = \frac{2c}{5}$ and $k = \frac{7c}{5}$.

Therefore:

$$\frac{g}{k} = \frac{\frac{2c}{5}}{\frac{7c}{5}} = \frac{2c}{5} \cdot \frac{5}{7c} = \frac{2}{7}$$
The answer is .2857.
